ocr.text: The prevalence of urinary disorders in feline populations has garnered significant attention in veterinary medicine, particularly
concerning conditions such as haemorrhagic cystitis. This disorder, characterized by inflammation and bleeding within the
urinary bladder, poses serious health risks and can lead to considerable discomfort for affected cats.
Disorders  Number of cats  Culture-positive cats
Haemorrhagic cystitis  8  2
Polypoid cystitis  3  -
Feline idiopathic cystitis  4  1
Transitional cell carcinoma (TCC)  2  -
Urethral plaque  14  4
Bladder stones  3  1
Urethritis  2  -
Acute Renal Failure  8  3
Chronic Renal Failure  6  2
Diabetes Mellitus  2  1
Other diseases  9  2
Understanding the epidemiology of such disorders is essential for effective diagnosis and treatment strategies. The data
presented in the table illustrates the distribution of various urinary disorders among a sample population of cats, highlighting
not only the total number of cats affected but also the subset of culture-positive cases. Specifically, the table indicates that out of
the total number of cats diagnosed with haemorrhagic cystitis, 8 were identified, with 2 of those cases confirmed as culture-
positive. This information is crucial, as culture-positive cases often indicate a bacterial infection that may require targeted
antibiotic therapy, whereas culture-negative cases may necessitate alternative treatment approaches. Analyzing these metrics
provides valuable insights into the underlying causes and potential treatment pathways for feline urinary disorders, ultimately
contributing to improved veterinary care and better outcomes for affected animals.
